text.title: AI in Project Management (PM-Agile) — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every PMBOK 8-aligned AI-PM adoption must enforce. These rules pin the discipline that converts AI assistance into an auditable PM tool rather than an autonomous decision-maker.

rule.statement: AI in PM operates in observation + synthesis mode only. Action triggers (budget reallocation, deadline change, risk closure, status update) are human-initiated and recorded with model + input-hash + reviewer.

rationale.source: PMBOK 8 AI appendix; PMI Code of Ethics

rule.rationale: An agent that auto-mutates the PM source of truth destroys auditability. PMBOK 8 explicitly requires human control for AI-assisted decisions.

rule.statement: Every AI-assisted decision is logged as a record containing: timestamp, model version, input data hash, output summary, human reviewer name, and approved/modified/rejected status with one-sentence rationale. Records are append-only.

rationale.source: ISO 31000 risk decision documentation; audit literature

rule.rationale: Without trace fields, regulators (and retros) cannot reconstruct why a decision was taken. Append-only logs preserve evidence even when the decision is later reversed.

rule.statement: Before any AI tool is wired into a PM ritual, the DORA four-key baseline (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR) is captured. AI adoption that has no baseline is rejected because it cannot demonstrate improvement.
rule.rationale: DORA 2025 shows AI coding assistants boost individual output but organizational delivery stays flat — bottlenecks shift to review and deployment. Without a baseline the paradox is invisible.

rule.statement: AI summaries that report "green" status across all metrics are flagged for explicit human cross-check against qualitative team signals (1:1 notes, channel sentiment). Green-only outputs are forbidden as final artefacts.
rule.rationale: AI-generated summaries can obscure nuance — a "green" risk status may hide a critical qualitative flag only a human catches. Forcing the cross-check converts an opaque green into a defensible one.

rule.statement: Model versions and prompts used in any production AI-PM workflow are pinned (model id + prompt SHA). Upgrades go through a documented change-record with regression check against the prior model's outputs on a labelled sample.
rule.rationale: A silent model upgrade can change risk classifications and digest tone enough to alter human decisions. Pinning + change-record prevents drift that looks like a sudden team behavioural shift.

example.title: Logged AI risk-score
example.code: trigger: weekly risk-register refresh model: claude-sonnet-4-7 (id=cls-4-7-20260322) prompt_sha: d4f1... input_hash: sha256:9ab... output_summary: 12 risks scored; 3 escalated to amber, 1 to red. reviewer: pm:alice decision: approved (the red is the data-pipeline outage from PR #321)
example.note: All required fields filled; reviewer cross-checked the red against the PR; record is append-only.

example.title: Auto-mutated status
example.code: agent: claude (no version pinned) action: closed 7 risks tagged "resolved" automatically audit_trail: (none)
example.note: Violates r1 (autonomous action), r2 (no trace fields), r5 (no model pinning). Reject.
